authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(42) — Deep Creek (San Bernardino Co.)
sources.quotedText: Deep Creek (San Bernardino Co.) from below Green Valley Lake Dam to the confluence of Willow Creek. All year. Only artificial lures may be used. 2 trout

sources.title: San Bernardino National Forest — Deep Creek Recreation Site
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 270792, "Deep Creek", Stream, San Bernardino Co., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":270792,"gaz_name":"Deep Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"San Bernardino"}; geometry multipoint, source point (Web Mercator -13029797.2959, 4060888.4799) converts to lon -117.048661, lat 34.238623, at the head of Little Green Valley below Green Valley Lake Dam — also cited by Wikipedia's {{cite gnis|270792}} for Deep Creek (34°14'19"N 117°02'55"W); ~3.48 km from the file's previous coordinate (-117.0850, 34.2300), which this GNIS-sourced value replaces.

parameters.note: Applies to Deep Creek from below Green Valley Lake Dam downstream to the confluence with Willow Creek.

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year, from below Green Valley Lake Dam to the confluence of Willow Creek
regulations.verbatimText: Deep Creek (San Bernardino Co.) from below Green Valley Lake Dam to the confluence of Willow Creek. All year.

parameters.note: Only artificial lures may be used.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures may be used (no bait)
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: 2 trout daily bag limit; this special regulation entry does not specify a distinct possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
